Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Stage 3 FFT results (ENOB=7.93) and Magic LVS flow

Stage 3 FFT (N=1024, M=499, Fin=48.7MHz near Nyquist):
- SNDR=49.51dB, SFDR=65.41dB, ENOB=7.93 bit
- All 3 stages confirm near-ideal 8-bit performance across full band

Magic VLSI tech file (ptm28nm.tech) for LVS extraction:
- Correct nfet/pfet identification via nwell discrimination in cifinput
- Fixed connect rules: FET types only in poly group (prevents gate-S/D short)
- End-to-end LVS flow: GDS → Magic extract → netgen comparison (TEST_INV passes)
- Includes test scripts, reference netlist, and netgen setup

Fix PyPI publish: use dynamic version from __init__.py

pyproject.toml had version hardcoded as 1.0.0 while __init__.py had
1.1.2, causing CI builds to always produce 1.0.0 (already on PyPI).
Switch to dynamic versioning and bump to 1.2.0.

commit.message:
Claude/fix level progression bug mx6 bm (#342)

* Add Illness Log as dedicated tool and integrate into workout decisions

1. ExploreTab: Added Illness Log as its own tool in the Body category
   (was previously buried as a keyword under Injury Log). Full
   description surfaces the Neck Check protocol, daily check-ins, and
   return-to-training phases.

2. Daily Directive: Active illness now gates the "should I train today?"
   decision. If the illness engine says no training (fever, systemic,
   below-neck, or GI symptoms), the directive forces rest with
   illness-specific headline, actions, and training modifications.
   Recovering athletes get RPE caps and duration limits.

3. Performance Engine: Added illness as an 11th readiness factor
   (weight 0.10). Active illness scores 5/100 (triggers critical limiter
   cap), recovering scores proportional to allowed volume. Recently
   resolved illness (within 14 days) continues to impact readiness
   during return-to-training phases. Existing factor weights rebalanced
   to accommodate.

Science: Weidner 1998 Neck Check Rule, Friman & Wesslén 2000 fever/
myocarditis risk, Schwellnus 2016 return-to-play protocol.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01FracW8c5FPGAWk1NcmX4LQ

* Fix next workout progression skipping back to W1/D1

The getNextWorkout logic (daily directive) and nextUpSession (WorkoutView)
both iterated weeks from W1 forward, returning the first uncompleted
session. If a user completed a later session (e.g. Deload Day 1) but
earlier sessions were uncompleted, it would jump back to W1/D1 instead
of continuing to Deload Day 2.

Fix: find the most recently completed session by log date, then search
forward from that position for the next uncompleted session. Only falls
back to "first uncompleted from start" for fresh mesocycles with no
completions.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01FracW8c5FPGAWk1NcmX4LQ

* Fix mobile viewport overflow and pinch-to-zoom issues

- Lock viewport scale to 1 and disable user-scalable (standard PWA behavior)
- Remove max-w-lg from tab bar that constrained it unnecessarily
- Add overflow-x-hidden + w-full to main content container and overlay wrapper
- Change ExploreTab pinned tools from 4-col to 3-col grid for better mobile fit

https://claude.ai/code/session_01FracW8c5FPGAWk1NcmX4LQ

---------

commit.message:
Add proper one-click unsubscribe endpoint for newsletter

RFC 8058 requires List-Unsubscribe-Post to point to a real POST
endpoint that immediately opts out without user interaction. Previously
it pointed to /konto which requires login — Gmail penalises this.

New GET /api/newsletter/unsubscribe?email=... for manual link clicks.
New POST /api/newsletter/unsubscribe?email=... for one-click from clients.
Each email now gets a per-recipient unsubscribe URL.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix OpenAI API calls to use max_completion_tokens

Newer OpenAI models (gpt-4o, etc.) require max_completion_tokens
instead of max_tokens. Updated all OpenAI-specific API calls in:
- OpenAIProvider.js (all 4 methods)
- AIService.js (fixWithOpenAI, fixWithOpenAICompatible, _chatComplete,
  OpenAI streaming transform)
- aiCompletionService.js (OpenAI completion)

Claude, Groq, and Mistral providers unchanged as they still use
max_tokens per their API specs.

commit.message:
Fix sign-in not working: use client-side auth instead of server-only API login

The sign-in page was calling /api/auth/login which created the session
server-side only. The client-side auth context never received the session,
so after redirect to /dashboard the user appeared unauthenticated.

Now uses the auth context's login method which does client-side
signInWithPassword, properly updating the auth state. Also adds
account existence check with sign-out on failure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Improve newsletter deliverability: List-Unsubscribe headers + plain-text version

Adds List-Unsubscribe / List-Unsubscribe-Post headers so Gmail and
Outlook show the unsubscribe button rather than routing to junk.
Adds a plain-text alternative (auto-stripped from HTML) and sets a
display From name 'Vorbasse Boldklub <email>'.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

polish: pre-deployment multi-dimension code audit fixes

P0 Bug fixes:
- Fix N+1 query in guide.py tasks_for_me (per-task claim_count → JOIN subquery)
- Add pagination metadata to /wallet/transactions (total, page, page_size)
- Add LIMIT 10000 to settlement batch query preventing OOM
- Add logger.debug to all 8 event_bus.publish() silent exception handlers

P1 API consistency & i18n:
- Replace 15 hardcoded Chinese strings in guide.py with English
- Add TaskCreate.difficulty validation (easy|medium|hard|expert)
- Add TaskCreate.category max_length=64
- Save DisputeResolveRequest.comment in resolution_comment column

P2 Code quality:
- Deduplicate Ed25519 auth logic in deps.py (reuse get_agent_by_signature)
- Cache Web3 instance as singleton in provider.py
- Wrap blocking Web3 calls in asyncio.to_thread (bridge + settlement)

P3 Security hardening:
- Make CORS origins configurable via MARKET_CORS_ORIGINS
- Gate settlement create endpoint behind Expert+ reputation (score >= 60)

P4 README corrections:
- Fix 6 wrong API paths (propose, endorse, rally, rally-status, refer, fair-shares)
- Fix reputation tier table (add Specialist, correct score ranges and limits)
- Document Ed25519 auth, skill versions, guide endpoints, MCP endpoints
- Update architecture diagram with new files

All 212 tests pass.

Fix invite flow, auth, security, scalability, and UI improvements

- Fix broken invite feature: align API schema to accept snake_case fields matching frontend
- Fix payout_method hardcoded to "cash" — now uses form selection
- Fix logout not working: add server-side cookie clearing + hard redirect
- Fix exchange rate API: rewrite from broken POST to GET with Supabase auth
- Add auth/role checks to 5 unprotected API routes (rooms, cam-accounts, shifts, support, ai)
- Fix CRON_SECRET bypass in trial reminders
- Add role-aware cut %: hide for accountants, show as "Studio Cut %" with tooltip for admin/owner
- Capitalize role badges in users table
- Add time-based pagination to data hooks for scalability
- Add 5 composite DB indexes for query performance
- Replace N+1 loops with batch operations in admin dashboard
- Optimize auth context SELECT queries
- Remove legacy _huf field fallbacks
- Fix dropdown capitalization and button text consistency

commit.message:
feat(yield): add rate-derived APY for dividend-distributing tokens

BUIDL and YLDS maintain a $1.00 NAV and distribute yield as new tokens,
so price-derived APY returns ~0%. Add a new rate-derived resolution tier
that computes APY from the already-cached T-bill rate (FRED DGS3MO)
minus each token's management fee spread.

Configured tokens: BUIDL (-20 bps), YLDS (-50 bps), USTB (-15 bps),
mTBILL (0 bps). No new API calls or cron jobs — piggybacks on the
existing fetch-tbill-rate pipeline.

commit.message:
Surface material system — 4 procedural materials with color + height textures

Replace single-channel grain LUT with full material system (board, canvas,
paper, gesso). Each material generates both a height texture (grain) and
color texture (material color with per-pixel variation). Compositor now
layers surface color between atmosphere and paint — scraping reveals warm
wood grain or cream canvas instead of empty sky.

commit.message:
refactor: unified personality-weighted AI scoring system (v4)

Replace priority-based chooseAction() in all 9 AI personas with shared
scoreAction() from new personality.js. Each AI defines weight multipliers
for action types (sets, attacks, buying, tome, etc.) instead of rigid
priority chains. Adds controlled noise via state.rng for human-like
variance. Fixes MCTS to use shared scoreAction instead of removed
ScoringAI.simulateAction(). Win rates now balanced 16-32% across all AIs.

commit.message:
fix(agent-mode): e2e fixes and Codex review findings

E2E fixes:
- Seed messages with initial user message (Claude API requires ≥1)
- Guard against null failure field (server returns failure: null)
- Goal: "Prefer nonstop flights when possible" — creates budget
  tradeoff without being too directive
- Use explicit date (2026-03-10)

Codex review fixes:
- Clear budget_failures after successful escalation so stale failure
  records can't mint multiple tokens
- Check token registration success in both --agent and scripted modes
  (registered: false → error)
- Fix requirements.txt comment (anthropic needed for --agent too)
